treatment_information.specific_treatment: Percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) with drug eluting stent
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Drug eluting stent (Cypher 3x13mm) placed in the native right coronary artery
treatment_information.treatment_response: No chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, or diaphoresis post-procedure

treatment_information.specific_treatment: Heparin gtt, Plavix, Aspirin, high dose statin, Atenolol

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with palpitations and chest discomfort
Stress test showed anterolateral wall ischemia and EF 52%
Patient ruled in for NSTEMI with peak troponin of 0.48
Started on heparin gtt, Plavix, Aspirin, high dose statin, Atenolol
Cardiac catheterization performed at outside hospital showing tight lesion in right coronary artery
Repeat catheterization resulted in drug eluting stent placement
Post-procedure, patient had no chest pain or other symptoms

patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Tolerated the procedure well and is now chest pain free
